ModuleName: Binary Trees: Structure, Traversal, and Operations

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics
Artifacts.Description: A structured outline of the module's core topics—covering binary tree fundamentals (nodes, edges, root), tree anatomy, BST ordering and node structure, insertion and search logic with JavaScript implementation, and tree traversal methods—used by learners to preview what will be taught and by instructors to verify full curriculum coverage.

Artifacts.Name: Module Topics (HTML)
Artifacts.Description: A navigational overview page listing all topics covered in the module—including binary tree definitions, terminology, structural rules, traversal methods, and core operations—that learners and instructors can use to see the full scope of the module and jump to specific sections.

Artifacts.Name: Introduction to Binary Trees
Artifacts.Description: This reading introduces the definition, terminology (root, parent, child, leaf), and structural rules of binary trees, giving learners the conceptual foundation needed before studying algorithms or specialized tree variants.

Artifacts.Name: Node Structure and Tree Anatomy
Artifacts.Description: This reading explains the anatomy of a binary tree at the node level — covering pointer structure, left/right child relationships, and how nodes link together — so learners can reason precisely about tree construction and manipulation.

Artifacts.Name: Binary Search Trees (BST) Concepts
Artifacts.Description: This reading defines the Binary Search Tree ordering property (left subtree holds smaller values, right holds larger), distinguishing BSTs from general binary trees, so learners understand the invariant that makes BST operations efficient.

Artifacts.Name: Insertion and Search Operations
Artifacts.Description: This reading walks through how insertion and search operations work in a BST by following the ordering invariant at each node, helping learners understand the recursive logic and efficiency of these two core operations.

Artifacts.Name: Deletion Operations
Artifacts.Description: This reading covers the three cases of BST node deletion — leaf node, one-child node, and two-child node (using in-order successor/predecessor) — so learners understand how to remove nodes without violating the BST ordering property.

Artifacts.Name: Tree Traversal Strategies
Artifacts.Description: This reading surveys tree traversal strategies — including in-order, pre-order, post-order, and level-order — explaining the visit sequence each produces and when each strategy is appropriate for a given task.

Artifacts.Name: Implementing a BST in JavaScript
Artifacts.Description: This reading guides learners through a complete from-scratch JavaScript implementation of a BST, including class and node structure and recursive algorithms, so they can translate conceptual BST knowledge into working code.

Artifacts.Name: Implementing a BST in JavaScript
Artifacts.Description: This reading guides learners through a complete from-scratch JavaScript implementation of a BST, including class and node structure and recursive algorithms, so they can translate conceptual BST knowledge into working code.
